Off Calendar Dec-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa Oberg, Esq., and Felicia Feng, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
James P. Nevin, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Lynn Patrick Schmidt

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect). Assuming without deciding that defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting admissible evidence sufficient to establish the first two elements of Boyle and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue on either of those elements, defendant did not present admissible evidence sufficient to establish the third element of Boyle. Plaintiffs' evidentiary objections 22-37 are sustained. The remaining issues are withdrawn. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Dana Companies, LLC
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Lynn Patrick Schmidt

RULING:
DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC (FORMERLY KNOWN AS SQUARE D)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he handled asbestos-containing Victor gaskets. Paragraph 8 of the declaration of Mr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue regarding plaintiff's exposure to asbestos fibers from the Victor gasket. Because there is no evidence that there is a material difference between automobile and boat gaskets on the issue of release of asbestos fibers from the handling of gaskets, the objection to paragraph 8 of Mr. Cohen's declaration is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
